REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS GRACE TANG, VERNON TANG, ALEX TANG, AND VICTORIA CORDEIRO'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE COMPLAINT AS ALLEGED DOES NOT ASSERT ANY CLAIMS ARISING OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO ANALYZE THE COMPLAINT AND ALLEGATIONS IN IT IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CONTROLLING LAW, BARAL V. SCHNITT (2016) 1 CAL.5TH 376. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT MOVING PARTIES FAILED TO IDENTIFY IN THEIR MOVING PAPERS ANY ALLEGATIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGEDLY BASED ON PROTECTED ACTIVITY. SUCH ATTEMPT IS MADE IN THE REPLY WITHOUT ANY ANALYSIS OR REFERENCE TO THE CONTROLLING AUTHORITY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs GRACE TANG, VERNON TANG, ALEX TANG, and VICTORIA CORDEIRO's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. The Complaint as alleged does not assert any claims arising out of protected activity. The Court notes that the moving party failed to analyze the complaint and allegations in it in accordance with the controlling law, Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376. The Court further notes that moving parties failed to identify in their moving papers any allegations in the First Amended Complaint allegedly based on protected activity. Such attempt is made in the reply without any analysis or reference to the controlling authority. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported (D501)
